overview: Mono-Red Goblins is a fast-paced aggressive deck that relies on tribal synergy and mana acceleration to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. By leveraging Legion Loyalist for early land drops and card advantage, the deck establishes a wide board of 1/1s quickly. Rundvelt Hordemaster and Goblin Warchief then multiply your combat damage, turning a swarm of goblins into an unstoppable offensive force in the Pioneer format.
win_conditions: Overwhelm the opponent with a wide board of low-cost goblins
Activate Goblin Warchief to pump attackers and close the game in combat
Use Legion Loyalist to accelerate mana and deploy threats ahead of curve

keep_criteria: Hands containing Legion Loyalist or Goblin Warchief
Hands with early land drops and aggressive creatures
Keep hands with 1-2 lands, at least two 1-drops (e.g., Goblin Guide, Goblin Racketeer, or a cheap pump), and a finisher or pump effect.
Drop hands with three or more lands, only a single 1-drop, or exclusively high-cost cards.
Prioritize hands that can play a 1-drop on turn 1 and a land + threat/pump on turn 2.

early_game: Play lands and one-mana goblins to establish board presence
Use Legion Loyalist to fetch additional lands and accelerate
Apply early pressure to force opponent to use removal inefficiently
Turn 1: Play a 1-drop goblin (preferably one with haste or an immediate impact).
Turn 2: Play a land, then either a 2-drop goblin or a pump effect like Legion Loyalist.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your pump spells on the same turn as your threats.
If you have Goblin Warchief, play it early to buff your board, then follow up with Legion Loyalist or Rundvelt Hordemaster for haste + +1/+1 synergy.
mid_game: Deploy Goblin Warchief to boost attack power and trigger combat tricks
Use Rundvelt Hordemaster to generate token goblins and widen the board
Push damage through while protecting key threats
Mana is your most critical resource
always keep open mana for pump spells or interaction, especially when the opponent taps out.
Hand is your primary resource—avoid overcommitting to the board without backup.
Graveyard is generally irrelevant unless you're running specific discard or sacrifice synergies.
Commit threats when you have pump support or when the opponent has no removal.
Hold back if they have sweepers or spot removal ready.
late_game: Maintain board control with a wide army of goblins
Use token generation to trigger Warchief and Hordemaster repeatedly
Close out the game with overwhelming combat damage
Rundvelt Hordemaster and Legion Loyalist with pump effects, or a fully pumped board of goblins.
Win by attacking with haste threats or chump blocking to preserve key pumpers.
Use pump spells to make threats too big to block.
When you have multiple pump effects, the opponent has no removal, or you're ahead on life total and can race.
Also commit when you can play a pump and a threat on the same turn to swing for lethal or massive damage.

key_cards.name: Legion Loyalist
key_cards.notes: Activate early to fetch lands, then use its draw ability to find win conditions like Warchief or Hordemaster.

key_cards.name: Rundvelt Hordemaster
key_cards.notes: Play when you have multiple goblins in hand to trigger its enter abilities, creating a wide board quickly.

key_cards.name: Goblin Warchief
key_cards.notes: Hold up mana to activate its ability before combat, boosting all goblins to close games or clear blockers.

sequencing_tips: Activate Legion Loyalist before playing other creatures to maximize land drops
Wait to play Goblin Warchief until you have multiple goblins ready to attack
Use Rundvelt Hordemaster's tokens to trigger Warchief's ability for maximum damage
Common mistakes: Overcommitting to board without pump support, ignoring life total, failing to sequence lands properly.
Unique interactions: Legion Loyalist and Rundvelt can be played on the same turn for massive haste pump.
Watch for 'Goblin Piledriver' or similar pump effects if format allows.
Always prioritize playing your pump effects before your threats to maximize damage.

matchups.advice: Race before they stabilize with sweepers or counterspells. Push damage early and use Warchief to bypass blockers.
Apply constant pressure to prevent them from playing their big threats. Use Hordemaster to generate value and outpace their curve.
Use Legion Loyalist to accelerate and match their speed. Prioritize wide boards over big pumps to survive combat trades.

Use cheap, efficient threats that can't be countered (if any), or overcommit with multiple threats. Use pump spells to make threats too big to ignore. If possible, use instant-speed interaction to clear the way.
Apply maximum pressure early. Don't let them stabilize. Use removal on their ramp sources if possible. If they have a big threat, trade your goblins efficiently to survive until you can close.

Value exchange. Use removal efficiently. Win by having better late-game threats or more pump effects. Track their hand for key cards like Legion Loyalist or Rundvelt, and prioritize trading your expendable goblins for their key pieces.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), spot removal (e.g., Path to Exile, Lightning Strike), and life gain.
Note when they tap out for a big spell or when they start drawing into answers.
Pay attention to their mana base for potential sweepers or removal.
